# Figure description: Страт_колонки_скважин_черт_49_л_4.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Страт_колонки_совмещенные_по_скважинам/Страт_колонки_скважин_черт_49_л_4.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **composite stratigraphic correlation diagram** (литологический/стратиграфический корреляционный разрез) showing multiple borehole/well lithological columns correlated laterally across a survey area. It is a standard Soviet-era geological documentation format combining individual well logs (геологические разрезы скважин) with inter-well stratigraphic correlation lines.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers multiple boreholes drilled across the northern Kazakhstan survey area corresponding to map sheets **M-42** and **N-42** (1:200,000 scale). The columns are arranged spatially to represent different structural zones, and correlation lines connect equivalent stratigraphic horizons between wells. The boreholes referenced appear to include references to:

- **Центральный разрез** (Central section) — referenced in the upper title area
- **Бачковский горизонт** (Bachkovsky horizon) — referenced in mid-column titles
- **Бозанбайский горизонт** (Bozanbaysky horizon) — visible in column headers
- **Скважина (скв.)** numbers are annotated alongside columns, though exact numbers are difficult to read at this resolution, but references to **скв. В-1024**, **скв. М-42**, and related designations appear in column headers
- **Сабалинский разрез** (Sabalinsky section) — visible in titles
- Масштаб (scale) references of **1:200,000** and **1:100,000** appear in headers

The depth ranges of individual columns appear to span from surface to approximately **500–1000+ metres** based on the vertical scale of the columns, though exact values require higher resolution to confirm.

---

## Key Content

The figure presents **7–9 vertical lithological columns** arranged from left to right, representing boreholes or composite sections distributed across the survey area. Key features include:

### Lithological Symbols (standard Soviet notation):
- **Dotted/stippled patterns**: sandstones (песчаники) — the dominant lithology visible across most columns
- **Horizontal line patterns**: clays or mudstones (глины/аргиллиты)
- **Brick/block patterns**: conglomerates or coarser clastic material (конгломераты/гравелиты) — visible at the base of several columns
- **Dark/solid fill**: coal seams or carbonaceous layers (угольные пласты/прослои) — visible as thin dark bands in the lower portions of multiple columns
- **Diagonal hatch marks (////)**: possibly fault zones, unconformities, or missing intervals — visible as diagonal cross-hatched symbols in the leftmost and some central columns
- **Wavy horizontal lines**: possible argillaceous or mixed lithologies

### Stratigraphic Correlation:
- Correlation lines connect equivalent beds between columns, showing **lateral continuity and pinch-out** of individual layers
- Some correlation lines converge or diverge, indicating **lateral facies changes** or thinning of units
- Several **marker horizons** (реперные горизонты) are highlighted with bold correlation lines, suggesting regionally traceable beds
- The correlation indicates a generally **subhorizontal to gently dipping** stratigraphy with some lateral variation

### Structural Features:
- The diagonal hatch symbols (likely representing **тектонические нарушения** — tectonic disturbances or faults) appear repeatedly in the leftmost column, suggesting a structurally disrupted zone on the western flank of the section
- Some columns show **breaks or unconformities** in the stratigraphic sequence

### Vertical Scale:
- Each column appears to have a graduated depth scale on its side; intervals appear to be marked at regular increments (likely 50m or 100m intervals based on column heights)
- Labels such as **"0,4г/см³"**, **"10м"**, **"100м"** type annotations appear beside some horizons

### Formation Labels:
- Index labels in the format **С₁**, **С₂**, **D**, or similar Cyrillic/geological index notation appear beside individual units within columns, corresponding to Carboniferous (C) and possibly Devonian (D) stratigraphic assignments
- Labels such as **"С₁ад"**, **"С₁вз"**, **"С₂бк"** type designations (sub-stage level Carboniferous stratigraphy) are visible
